key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar boosts copper particle velocity past the bonding threshold, producing denser deposits with better conductivity and much higher ductility.
engineering_relevance: This defines a practical process window for CSAM copper parts where mechanical integrity and electrical performance can be balanced for functional manufacturing.
method: LightSPEE3D cold spray copper deposits were produced at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar.
Microstructure and phase-state trends were assessed by XRD and SEM.
Performance was measured using hardness, eddy-current conductivity, and tensile testing.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a clear pressure-dependent trend: higher pressure increased crystallite refinement, improved inter-particle bonding and conductivity, and shifted tensile behavior toward ductile fracture.
watch_item: Higher pressure improved ductility but reduced hardness, so the best setting depends on whether the priority is conductivity/ductility or surface wear resistance.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
